title: Type aliases should be declared with a type statement
description: Global type aliases should have an explicit type statement.
message: Global type alias declaration without type statement.
impact: Readability and maintainability may be impacted.
recommendation: Add the type statement to the global type alias declaration.

noncompliant.description: Global type variable used without a type statement.

compliant.code:
T = TypeVar('T')
from typing import TypeVar
def function(arg: T) -> T:
    return arg + 1
compliant.description: Global type variable with explicit type statement.

why_is_this_an_issue: The lack of an explicit type statement can make the code less readable and harder to understand.
how_to_fix: Add an explicit type statement for global type aliases.

checks.message: Global type alias declaration without type statement.

metadata.title: Type aliases should be declared with a type statement
